sources: Unrealized Gains on Berkshire Hathaway Holdings

philanthropy.name: Planned Stock Grants to Charitable Trusts

2026.methodology: 2026 forecast assumes a conservative 10% CAGR on the $148.9B net worth reported in early Jan 2026, offset by a projected $6.5B philanthropic grant in June (continuing the 4% annual share reduction policy). Total income represents the net change in asset valuation minus realized losses. Personal compensation is adjusted to near-zero following his retirement from the CEO role effective Jan 1, 2026.

official_compensation.name: Fixed CEO Salary and Security Costs

charitable_contributions.name: Annual Stock Donation to Five Foundations

2025.methodology: Income reflects the net increase in wealth from approximately $144.1B (Jan 2025) to $166.1B (Dec 2025, est. Bloomberg/Forbes real-time indices) adjusted for mid-year philanthropic distributions. Fixed compensation remains $100,000 salary plus ~$305,111 in security costs. Spending is primarily the record $6 billion donation of 12.36M Class B shares announced in June 2025.

sources: Net Worth Appreciation (Berkshire Hathaway Class A & B Shares)

2024.methodology: Income calculated as the delta in Forbes/Bloomberg Net Worth between Jan 1, 2024 ($119B) and Dec 31, 2024 (est. $132B), plus exact SEC-filed compensation of $405,111. Spending is primarily defined by the record $5.3 billion donation of Class B shares to five foundations announced in June 2024.
